observations.recommendation: 3. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require all the depository banks to strictly comply with the submission of the mandated bank statements (BS) and supporting documents for all depository accounts maintained by the Municipality within five days after the end of each month. In turn, within ten days from the receipts of such BS, the same should be reconciled with the General Ledgers which shall be the basis for preparation of BRS; and henceforth, the BRS must be regularly submitted to the Auditor with Bank Statements and/or Snapshots within ten days after the end of each month together with the paid checks and debit/credit memos. Likewise, prepare the necessary journal entry voucher (JEVs) to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.

11. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, direct the Municipal Accountant to timely submit the Statement of Receipts and Disbursements and related supporting documents to the source agencies to avoid non-reconciliation of balances as well as to avoid the risk that they may not be able to be given additional funding from the National Government.

12. We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of MOA/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the NGAs to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

15. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the personnel concerned to submit the list of PPAs of the Municipality, annually at the beginning of the year; and prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure projects in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

28. Required the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD data-base with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.

1.10 We reiterate our recommendations that the Accountable Officers:
a. Determine and address all the reasons for the delay in the submission
of the required reports and if the delay is caused by accountable
officer’s negligence consider imposing sanctions for the delayed
submission of the financial reports and supporting documents to the
Office of the Auditor, if any. Further review and evaluate the
performance of the finance personnel; and recommend replacement
for those found undesirable with qualified personnel with the right
work attitude;
b. Conduct planning, target setting and prepare commitment reports
particularly on the timeliness on the submission of transaction
documents/reports and financial statements provided in Section 7.2.1
(a) of COA Circular 2009-006 and Section 107 of PD 1445; and
c. Office of the Local Chief Executive conduct periodic monitoring of
submission of required reports to COA, DBM and other regulatory
bodies and see to it that all the required reports are submitted on time.

2.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require all the
depository banks to strictly comply with the submission of the mandated
bank statements (BS) and supporting documents for all depository
accounts maintained by the Municipality within five (5) days after the end
of each month. In turn, within 10 days from the receipts of such BS, the
same should be reconciled with the General Ledgers which shall be the
basis for preparation of BRS; and henceforth, the BRS must be regularly
submitted to the Auditor with Bank Statements and/or Snapshots within
10 days after the end of each month together with the paid checks and
debit/credit memos. Likewise, prepare the necessary journal entry
voucher (JEVs) to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.

3.5 We reiterated this recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish at the year a certified list
of taxpayers with amount due and collectible for the year to the
Municipal Accountant based on the Real Property Tax Account
Register/ Taxpayer’s Index Card and make an effort to collect all
taxes due to the LGU.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use this as a basis in recording
Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax
Receivable; set up the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax
to present a more reliable and accurate balance.

4.11 We recommended that the agency should instruct the Inventory
Committee to conduct an actual physical count of all its PPE by listing all
the PPE items whether included in the latest Report on Physical Count of
PPE/ Physical Inventory Report or not and compare it to all PPE Ledger
Cards. This will be the basis in preparing the Physical Inventory Plan.
Reconcile the balances derived by the Inventory Committee Report with
the Accounting Records and take appropriate actions depending on the
status of the PPE. Further, apply the procedures in the derecognition of
non-existing/missing PPEs pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006 or the
One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies.

5.7 We recommended that the Property Custodian regularly
prepare/renew/update the AREs of the PPEs every three years or as soon
as there is a change in custody of accountability to strengthen control and
accountability over the government assets.

6.10 We therefore recommended that Management conduct an extensive
review and adjustment of the PPE and Inventory Accounts of the Agency
and adopt the provisions of the COA Circular 2022-004.

7.5 We recommended that Management instruct the AOs to (a) maintain the
records and registers as cited under the Revised Cash Examination
Manual and as presented in the Volume II of MNGAS for LGUs for more
effective and efficient monitoring and control of funds and for proper
accounting and auditing of related accounts thereafter in lieu of the cashbooks being
maintained; and (b) to record all transactions of cash in bank including
receipts and issuances of checks, record cancelled checks and debit and
credit memos in the checkbooks.

8.4 We therefore recommended that the AOs prepare and regularly submit
the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms with and
without money value to the Audit Team using the format provided in
Chapter 2, Volume II of the MNGAS for LGUs to ensure compliance with
the afore-cited regulation and to facilitate inventory of accountable forms.

9.9 We therefore recommended that management notify the Audit Team at
least three (3) days before there is an acquisition, delivery, inspection and
acceptance activities of the agency and to submit the necessary inventory
reports to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.

10.7 We recommended and Management agreed that to follow the provisions
of COA Circular 2021-001 pertaining to the proper usage of RER and
Certification in relation to reimbursement of expenses not requiring ORs
with the proper guidance of COA Circular 2012-001 Prescribing the Revised
Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government
Transactions.

11.5 We recommended that management instruct the BAC and Procurement
Support Unit to stop the practice of submitting the contract and
supporting documents together with the related disbursement vouchers,
instead furnished the Auditor copy of the contract and other supporting
documents not later than five (5) working days from issuance of purchase
orders and perfection of contracts as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA
Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

12.4 We recommended that Management furnish the Audit Team with the
copies of MOA/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the NGAs to
evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose and
as required under Item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June
14, 2012.

13.5 We recommended that management:
a. Submit to the Auditor’s Office a complete list of all on-going
projects, programs and activities and those that are to be
implemented during the year and should be updated every quarter
following the format prescribed by COA Circular 2013-004, dated
January 13, 2014 at the start of the year; and
b. Maintain the project status in Annex A of COA Cirular 2013-004 as
current as possible and update using the schedule stated above based
on the data sourced from monthly monitoring report prepared by
the agency and verified by the Technical Audit Specialist of the
Commission.

14.6 We recommend that the management will publicize not only the
infrastructure PPAs but also the non-infrastructure pursuant to COA
Circular 2016-003.

15.6 We therefore recommend that the agency refrain from disbursing
government funds for services not yet rendered or goods not yet delivered
as in the case of the advance payment of supplier. Failure to comply will
lead to the suspension or disallowance of such expenditure.

16.7 We recommend that management should:
a. Investigate the cause of the delay in the implementation of projects, if the
delay cannot be validly justified and if found out that fault is on the
management personnel, such concerned personnel should be subjected
to appropriate administrative action but if it is contractors’ fault, then
liquidated damages should be imposed upon latter or recission or
termination of contract if the slippage equals to 15% of the contract
amounts.
b. Immediately start the project after issuance of notice to proceed.
c. Exert more effort to finish these projects. If necessary, prepare a catch-
up plan to address the delay.
d. Refrain from awarding the contract to contractors with history of delays
in implementation of projects on the ground of incompetence.

17.7 We recommend that the agency prepare a meticulous and judicious APP
and PPMP in compliance with Section 7 of the RA 9184. APP must include
only planned procurement activities for Common-Use supplies and
Equipment (APP-CSE), Infrastructure Projects and other procured items
that is considered crucial to the efficient discharge of government
function. All Procurement shall be within the approved APP of the
agency’s PAPs.

18.5 We therefore recommended that management conduct planning
conferences to ensure that all the buildings and other structures of the
Agency are friendly to differently-abled persons/PWDs, and include the
same in its budget pursuant to BP Blg 344 in order to address their rights
to participate fully in the social life and the development of the societies
within the Agency.

19.8 We recommended that Management make representation with the
Sangguniang Bayan for the creation and installation of the Internal Audit
Service (IAS) directly under the Office of the Municipal Mayor to comply
with the requirement of AO Nos. 278 and 70 and ensure the quality of
performance and compliance with laws, rules and regulations.

20.8 We recommended that Management require the Municipal
Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require
insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS and furnished a copy to
the Audit Team for review and verification.
Further, we recommended that management insure all insurable assets
with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the
properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in
accordance with to RA 656.

21.4 We recommended that the Management prepare the PIF on or before
April 30 of each that could be extracted from RPCPPE and RPCI and
submitted the same to the Audit Team and GSIS to secure the inclusion in
the GIF.

23.7 We recommended that the LGU establish GAD database to contain sex
disaggregated data to serve as source of information for the conduct of
gender analysis, identify gender issues and determine gender responsive
PPAs and incorporate them in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.

24.6 We recommended that furnish a copy of the 20% DF report to the Office
of the Auditor and ensure that the utilization of the 20% DF be limited to
PPAs that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in
accordance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1 and refrain from
appropriating or including in the appropriation of development projects the
personnel services, not allowable to be charged to the 20% DF.

1. We recommended that the Accountable Officers conduct periodic monitoring of submission of required reports to COA, Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and other regulatory bodies and see to it that all required reports are submitted on time.

2. We recommended that the management submit a complete set of financial statements condensed and by fund cluster including the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as required under Chapter 19 Financial Reporting, Section 5 of GAM Volume I and Section 41 Paragraph 2 of PD 1445.

8. We recommended that the management mark all motor vehicles of the Municipality with “For Official Use Only” and to exert an effort that existing motor vehicles which are not yet government plated be registered accordingly under which place the name of the Municipality.

9. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish at the year a certified list of taxpayers with amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/ Taxpayer’s Index Card and make an effort to collect all taxes due to the LGU.

10. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use this as a basis in recording Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable; adjust the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax to present a more reliable and accurate balance.

13. We have recommended furnishing the Auditor copy of the POs and contract documents not later than five (5) working days from perfection and issuance of contracts together with duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation.

14. We therefore recommended that the management include the Audit Team in the acquisition, delivery, inspection and acceptance activities of the agency and the submit the necessary inventory reports to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.

16. We recommended that the Management should instruct the HRMO Department to submit current plantilla of personnel every year and furnish the Audit Team the copies of agency personnel movements (i.e. approved appointment/contract of service of every newly hired, promoted as well as the newly renewed contract) beforehand.

17. We recommended that Management should require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS and furnished a copy to the Audit Team for review and verification.

18. Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

19. Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare on time the annual GPB for review and approval by DILG and submit the approved copy and the corresponding AR to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and

20. Approve charges and disbursements on GAD Fund only if supported with DILG reviewed and approved GPB to ascertain validity and propriety of disbursements.

21. We recommended that the LDRRMO submit to the NDRRMC through the OCD the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF on or before the 5th day following the end of each month and furnish copy to the audit team for audit/evaluation.

22. Furnish a copy of the 20% DF report to the Office of the Auditor and ensure that the utilization of the 20% DF be limited to PPAs that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in accordance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1 and refrain from appropriating or including in the appropriation of development projects the personnel services, not allowable to be charged to the 20% DF.

27. Create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and reconciled balances and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due and surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contributions should be held liable and appropriate charges should be filed.

3. Create an Inventory Committee composed of at least three (3) members to conduct periodic physical count of all its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and as a report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.
4. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards and conduct regular reconciliation of records with the Municipal Accountant to ensure correctness.

9. The Municipal Assessor should update the Master List of Real Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.
13. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.

14. Required the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.
17. We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

We recommended that the Accountable Officers conduct periodic monitoring
of submission of required reports to COA, Department of Budget and Management
(DBM) and other regulatory bodies and see to it that all the required reports are
submitted on time.

We recommended that the management submit a complete set of financial
statements condensed and by fund cluster including the Statement of Comparison of
Budget and Actual Amounts as required under Chapter 19 Financial Reporting,
Section 5 of GAM Volume I and Section 41 Paragraph 2 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require all the depository
banks to strictly comply with the submission of the mandated bank statements (BS)
and supporting documents for all depository accounts maintained by the
Municipality within five days after the end of each month. In turn, within ten days
from the receipts of such BS, the same should be reconciled with the General
Ledgers which shall be the basis for preparation of BRS; and henceforth, the BRS
must be regularly submitted to the Auditor with Bank Statements and/or Snapshots
within ten days after the end of each month together with the paid checks and
debit/credit memos. Likewise, prepare the necessary journal entry voucher (JEVs)
to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.

We recommended the Management to require:
a) The Inventory Committee to conduct thorough review and physical count of
the inventories and PPE by establishing its actual conditions and
whereabouts, and come up with the RPCI and RPCPPE;
b) The Inventory Committee to adhere with the submission of RPCI for the first
and second semesters not later than July 31 and January 31, respectively,
and the annual RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to the Audit
Team for subsequent review;
c) The Supply and Property Unit and Accounting Office to strengthen its
system of maintaining its accounting and property records such as SCs, PCs,
SLCs, PPELCs, RPCI and RPCPPE to facilitate periodic reconciliation of
these records to ensure reliability of the inventories and PPE account
balances as presented in the Financial Statements of the Municipality.
d) The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for
the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer
should ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated
Property Card for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus
facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the
basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation
expenses pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its
appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to
present fairly the PPE accounts which its corresponding Accumulated
Depreciation Expense Accounts in FS.

We recommended that the management mark all motor vehicles of the Municipality
with “For Official Use Only” and to exert an effort that existing motor vehicles
which are not yet government plated be registered accordingly under which place the
name of the Municipality.

We reiterate this recommendation that the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish at the year a certified list of
taxpayers with amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal
Accountant based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/ Taxpayer’s
Index Card and make an effort to collect all taxes due to the LGU.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use this as a basis in recording Real
Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable; adjust the
Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax to present a more reliable and
accurate balance.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, direct the Municipal
Accountant to timely submit the Statement of Receipts and Disbursements and
related supporting documents to the source agencies to avoid non-reconciliation of
balances as well as to avoid the risk that they may not be able to be given additional
funding from the National Government.

We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the
copies of MOA/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the NGAs to evaluate
whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

We have recommended furnishing the Auditor copy of the POs and contract
documents not later than five (5) working days from perfection and issuance of
contracts together with duly accomplished supporting documents for technical
review and evaluation.

We therefore recommended that the management to include the Audit Team
in the acquisition, delivery, inspection and acceptance activities of the agency and
the submit the necessary inventory reports to the Office of the Auditor within the
reglementary period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the personnel
concerned to submit the list of PPAs of the Municipality, annually at the beginning
of the year; and prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure
projects in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that the Management should instruct the HRMO
Department to submit current plantilla of personnel every year and furnish the
Audit Team copies of agency personnel movements (i.e. approved
appointment/contract of service of every newly hired, promoted as well as the newly
renewed contract) beforehand.

We recommended that Management should require the Municipal
Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require
insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS and furnished a copy to the
Audit Team for review and verification.

Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to
fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No. 656,
otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245 dated July
13, 1973.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare on time the annual GPB for
review and approval by DILG and submit the approved copy and the
corresponding AR to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period
provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and
b) Approve charges and disbursements on GAD Fund only if supported with
DILG reviewed and approved GPB to ascertain validity and propriety of
disbursements.

We recommended that the LDRRMO submit to the NDRRMC through the
OCD the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF on or before
the 5th day following the end of each month and furnish copy to the audit team for
audit/evaluation.

Furnish a copy of the 20% DF report to the Office of the Auditor and ensure
that the utilization of the 20% DF be limited to PPAs that partake the nature of
investment or capital expenditures in accordance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1
and refrain from appropriating or including in the appropriation of development
projects the personnel services, not allowable to be charged to the 20% DF.

1. We recommended that the required monthly fund utilization reports and status of implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format be immediately posted in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality.
2. We recommended that management submit the distribution lists for verification and validation purposes.

10. Create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of balances and unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contributions should be held liable and appropriate charges should be filed.

11. The LGU should adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording the purchase of supplies and materials. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should prepare and maintain records and reports as required by the NGAS Manual.

15. At the beginning of the year, provide the Audit Team a list of all ongoing/government PPAs during the year. And conduct regular monitoring of all ongoing projects implemented by the municipality and prepare monthly accomplishment reports as to the status of implementation and submit regularly to the Audit Team the Quarterly Accomplishment Report following the format in Annex B of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

16. Require the HRMO to conduct periodic review and render report to the Local Chief Executive on the workload assigned specifically to casual/contractual employees and consultants and assessment on the necessity of the continuance of their employment with the LGU.

18. The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the timely submission of reports to facilitate the recording of the same in the books of accounts and submission to the Auditor’s Office within ten (10) days after receipt thereof.
19. The LGU should procure commonly used goods, supplies and materials from the Procurement Service in compliance to Administrative Order No. 17, series of 2011.

• Create an Inventory Committee composed of at least three (3) members to conduct periodic physical count of all its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.
• Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards and conduct regular reconciliation of records with the Municipal Accountant to ensure correctness.

The Municipal Assessor should update the Master List of Real Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contributions should be held liable and appropriate charges should be filed.

• The LGU should adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording purchase of supplies and materials.
• The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should prepare and maintain records and reports as required by the NGAS Manual.
Submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA within the legal time frame.

Require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.
We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.
The LGU should procure commonly used goods, supplies and materials from the Procurement Service in compliance with Administrative Order No. 17, series of 2011.
observations.managementAction: Management asserted that it would be economical if it purchase from local stores. Prices are almost at par with those sold at PS-DBM in Zamboanga City.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the required monthly fund utilization reports and status of
implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format be immediately posted in the
LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality.

We recommended that Management submit the distribution lists for verification
and validation purposes.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Create an Inventory Committee composed of at least three (3) members to
conduct periodic physical count of all its property, plant and equipment (PPE)
and a report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor.
Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped
from the books.
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards and conduct regular
reconciliation of records with the Municipal Accountant to ensure correctness.

We recommended that:
a. the Municipal Accountant obtain the necessary data from the Municipal
Engineer, then prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards for Local Road
Network, provide depreciation separately to each component of the road
network, submit a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation, and
disclose the road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements in
accordance with the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2015-008;
b. the Municipal Mayor constitute a committee to conduct inventory of all road
networks of the Municipality;
c. the Municipal Mayor tap other qualified personnel to fill-in the shoe of a
regular GSO or create the position of GSO if funds warrant it; and
d. the Municipal Engineer provide the local accountant and the Supply Officer
with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for
road projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor update the Master List of Real
Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the
preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due
and collectible for the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal
Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section
20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a committee to undertake
the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the
GSIS on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a
commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those
officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contributions should be held
liable and appropriate charges should be filed.

We recommended that:
a. The LGU adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording purchase of
supplies and materials.
b. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer prepare and maintain records and
reports as required by the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the Report on Sources
and Utilization of LDRRMF within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.

Require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with
adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the
bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.

At the beginning of the year, provide the Audit Team a list of all on-going/to be implemented government PPAs during the year. And conduct regular monitoring of all on-going projects implemented by the municipality and prepare monthly accomplishment reports as to the status of implementation and submit regularly to the Audit Team the Quarterly Accomplishment Report following the format in Annex B of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: List of PPAs complied during the four (4) quarters of CY 2019 were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: Require the HRMO to conduct periodic review and render report to the Local Chief Executive on the workload assigned specifically to casual/contractual employees and consultants and assessment on the necessity of the continuance of their employment with the LGU.

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the timely submission of reports to facilitate the recording of the same in the books of accounts and submission to the Auditor’s Office within ten (10) days after receipt thereof.
The LGU should submit its GAD Accomplishment Report/s to facilitate timely evaluation on the implementation of GAD related programs/projects and substantive audit of GAD related transactions.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has submitted the GAD Accomplishment Report but quite late.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that signboards be installed for all programs, projects and activities (PPAs) of the LGU.

We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books. We also recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and to conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to ensure correctness.

The Municipal Mayor should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contribution should be answerable for their actions and appropriate charges should be filed.

The Municipal Assessor should update the Master List of Real Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.
Submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA within the legal time frame.

Require the HRMO to conduct periodic review and render report to the Local Chief Executive on the workload assigned specifically to casual/contractual employees and consultants and assessment on the necessity of the continuance of their employment with the LGU.
We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.
The LGU should procure commonly used goods, supplies and materials from the Procurement Service in compliance with Administrative Order No. 17, series of 2011.
observations.reason: Management asser- ted that it would be economical if it purchase from local stores. Prices are almost at par with those sold at PS-DBM in Zamboanga City.

observations.recommendation: The LGU should adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording purchase of supplies and materials. The LCE should direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to prepare and maintain records and reports as required by the NGAS Manual to ensure that the correct amount of inventory and expense accounts are taken up in the books.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
• Create an Inventory Committee composed of at least three (3) members to
conduct periodic physical count of all its property, plant and equipment (PPE)
and a report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor.
Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped
from the books.
• Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards and conduct
regular reconciliation of records with the Municipal Accountant to ensure
correctness.

The Municipal Assessor should update the Master List of Real Property Taxpayers
to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of a
certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for
the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting
up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual,
Volume I.

Create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to
hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter,
they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding
premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory
contributions should be held liable and appropriate charges should be filed.

• The LGU should adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording purchase
of supplies and materials.
• The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should prepare and maintain records
and reports as required by the NGAS Manual.

Submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA within the legal
time frame.

Require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with
adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the
bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.

At the beginning of the Audit year, provide the Audit Team a list of all on-going/to be implemented government PPAs during the year. And conduct regular monitoring of all on-going projects implemented by the municipality and prepare monthly accomplishment reports as to the status of implementation and submit regularly to the Audit Team the Quarterly Accomplishment Report following the format in Annex B of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the timely submission of reports to facilitate the recording of the same in the books of accounts and submission to the Auditor’s Office within ten (10) days after receipt thereof.
The LGU should submit its GAD Accomplishment Report/s to facilitate timely evaluation on the implementation of GAD related programs/projects and substantive audit of GAD related transactions.
observations.reason: CY 2016 submitted the GAD Accomplishment Report but quite late.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that signboards be installed for all programs, projects and activities (PPAs) of the LGU.

We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year . Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.
We also recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and to conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to ensure correctness.

The Municipal Mayor should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contribution should be answerable for their actions and appropriate charges should be filed.

The Municipal Assessor should update the Master List of Real Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.
Submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA within the legal time frame.
We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.
The LGU should submit its GAD Accomplishment Report/s to facilitate timely evaluation on the implementation of GAD related programs/projects and substantive audit of GAD related transactions.

Require the HRMO to conduct periodic review and render report to the Local Chief Executive on the workload assigned specifically to casual/contractual employees and consultants and assessment on the necessity of the continuance of their employment with the LGU.
The LGU should procure commonly used goods, supplies and materials from the Procurement Service in compliance with Administrative Order No. 17, series of 2011.

Management should comply with the maximum limit of government share for Pag-IBIG Contribution based on the two (2) % of P 5,000 or P 100 per employee per month pursuant to the provisions of RA 9679 and RA 10964, and cause the refund from the employees concerned of the over remittance of Pag-BIG employer’s share premiums.

Recommendation:

   We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic  physical
   inventory of its property, plant and equipment (PPE) and a report thereon
   be immediately prepared and submitted  to  the  Auditor  not  later  than
   January 31 of each year .  Items that are beyond economic repair shall be
   properly disposed and dropped from the books.


   We also recommend that  the  LCE  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  and
   Municipal Treasurer to maintain PPE  Ledger  Cards  and  Property  Cards,
   respectively, and to conduct regularly reconciliation of their records to
   ensure correctness.

Recommendation:


   The  Municipal  Mayor  should  create  a  committee  to   undertake   the
   reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation  with
   the GSIS on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they  should  come
   up with a commitment on how  to  remit  the  long  outstanding  premiums.
   However, those officials responsible  for  not  remitting  the  mandatory
   contribution should be  answerable  for  their  actions  and  appropriate
   charges should be filed.

Recommendation:

   Management should comply with the maximum limit of government  share  for
   Pag-IBIG Contribution based on the two (2%) of  P  5,000  or  P  100  per
   employee per month pursuant to the provisions of RA 9679  and  RA  10964,
   and cause the refund from the employees concerned of the over  remittance
   of Pag-BIG employer’s share premiums.


   Management Comment:

   In the exit conference, management conformed to the audit observation and
     agreed to cause the refund from the employees concerned.

Recommendation:


   The Municipal Assessor should update the Master  List  of  Real  Property
   Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an  accurate  basis  in  the
   preparation of a certified list showing the names of  taxpayers  and  the
   amount due and collectible for the year.  The certified list shall be the
   basis of  the  Municipal  Accountant  in  setting  up  the  RPT  and  SET
   Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Recommendation:

   The LGU should maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the  20%
   Development Fund and 5% LDRRMF.

Recommendation:


   Submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA within  the
   legal time frame.

The Municipal Mayor should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contribution should be answerable for their actions and appropriate charges should be filed.

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the timely submission of reports to facilitate the recording of the same in the books of accounts and submission to the Auditor’s Office within ten (10) days after receipt thereof.
We recommend that signboards be installed for all programs, projects and activities (PPAs) of the LGU.

Require the LGU to establish Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) in the municipality and a Barangay Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (BDRRMO) in every barangay which shall be responsible for setting the direction, development, implementation and coordination of disaster risk management programs within their territorial jurisdiction.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has establish the LDRRMO

observations.recommendation: The management should observe strictly the provi-sions of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 and Revenue Memorandum Order No. 8-2003 on the withholding of value added tax, percentage tax, income tax and such other taxes that may arise on all government money payments.

The management require every department or end-user unit of the LGU to prepare Project Procure-ment Management Plan (PPMP) on their different programs, activities, and projects (PAPs) for consolidation into APP by the BAC.
